instruction: What is a zero of a polynomial? Give an example.
response: A real number k is called a zero of a polynomial p(x) if p(k) = 0. For example, consider p(x) = x² – 3x – 4. When we substitute x = –1: p(–1) = (–1)² – 3(–1) – 4 = 1 + 3 – 4 = 0. So –1 is a zero of this polynomial. Similarly, p(4) = 16 – 12 – 4 = 0, so 4 is also a zero. Zeroes are important because they tell us where the graph of the polynomial crosses the x-axis.

instruction: What is the geometrical meaning of the zeroes of a polynomial?
response: The zeroes of a polynomial p(x) are the x-coordinates of the points where the graph of y = p(x) intersects the x-axis. For a linear polynomial, the graph is a straight line that cuts the x-axis at exactly one point — giving exactly one zero. For a quadratic polynomial, the parabola can cut the x-axis at two distinct points (two zeroes), touch it at one point (one repeated zero), or not cut it at all (no real zeroes). A polynomial of degree n has at most n zeroes.

instruction: What are the different types of polynomials based on degree? Give one example of each.
response: Polynomials are classified by their degree as follows: Degree 1 is called a linear polynomial — example: 2x – 3. Degree 2 is called a quadratic polynomial — example: x² – 3x – 4. Degree 3 is called a cubic polynomial — example: x³ – 4x. In general, a quadratic polynomial has the form ax² + bx + c where a ≠ 0, and a cubic polynomial has the form ax³ + bx² + cx + d where a ≠ 0.

instruction: Find the zeroes of the quadratic polynomial 2x² – 8x + 6 and verify the relationship between zeroes and coefficients.
response: Factorise: 2x² – 8x + 6 = 2(x – 1)(x – 3). So zeroes are 1 and 3. Verification: Sum of zeroes = 1 + 3 = 4 = –(–8)/2 = –(coefficient of x)/(coefficient of x²) ✓. Product of zeroes = 1 × 3 = 3 = 6/2 = constant term/coefficient of x² ✓. This confirms the relationship: for ax² + bx + c, sum of zeroes = –b/a and product of zeroes = c/a.

instruction: How many zeroes can a quadratic polynomial have? Explain with geometrical reasoning.
response: A quadratic polynomial can have at most 2 zeroes. Geometrically, the graph of a quadratic polynomial is a parabola. There are three cases: Case 1 — the parabola cuts the x-axis at two distinct points, giving two distinct zeroes. Case 2 — the parabola touches the x-axis at exactly one point, giving two equal (repeated) zeroes. Case 3 — the parabola is entirely above or below the x-axis and does not intersect it, giving no real zeroes. So a quadratic polynomial has 0, 1, or 2 zeroes.

instruction: What is the zero of the linear polynomial ax + b? Derive it.
response: Let k be the zero of p(x) = ax + b. Then p(k) = 0, which gives ak + b = 0. Solving: ak = –b, so k = –b/a. Therefore, the zero of the linear polynomial ax + b is –b/a, which equals –(constant term)/(coefficient of x). For example, the zero of 2x + 3 is –3/2. This shows that the zero of a linear polynomial is directly related to its coefficients.

instruction: Why are expressions like 1/x and √x not polynomials?
response: A polynomial must have only non-negative integer powers of the variable. The expression 1/x = x⁻¹ has a negative power (–1), which is not allowed in a polynomial. The expression √x = x^(1/2) has a fractional power, which is also not allowed. Similarly, 1/(x+1) is not a polynomial. Polynomials only allow terms like x⁰, x¹, x², x³, and so on — whole number powers with real number coefficients.

instruction: बहुपद के शून्यक क्या होते हैं? उदाहरण सहित समझाइए।
response: किसी बहुपद p(x) का शून्यक वह वास्तविक संख्या k होती है जिसे x की जगह रखने पर बहुपद का मान शून्य हो जाता है, यानी p(k) = 0। उदाहरण: p(x) = x² – 3x – 4 के लिए, p(–1) = 1 + 3 – 4 = 0, इसलिए –1 एक शून्यक है। इसी प्रकार p(4) = 16 – 12 – 4 = 0, इसलिए 4 भी एक शून्यक है। ज्यामितीय दृष्टि से, शून्यक वे x-निर्देशांक हैं जहाँ बहुपद का ग्राफ x-अक्ष को काटता है।

instruction: द्विघात बहुपद के शून्यकों और गुणांकों में क्या संबंध होता है?
response: यदि α और β किसी द्विघात बहुपद ax² + bx + c के शून्यक हों, तो: शून्यकों का योग = α + β = –b/a = –(x का गुणांक)/(x² का गुणांक)। शून्यकों का गुणनफल = αβ = c/a = (अचर पद)/(x² का गुणांक)। उदाहरण: 2x² – 8x + 6 के शून्यक 1 और 3 हैं। योग = 1 + 3 = 4 = 8/2 ✓। गुणनफल = 1 × 3 = 3 = 6/2 ✓।

instruction: किसी बहुपद के शून्यकों का ज्यामितीय अर्थ क्या है?
response: बहुपद p(x) के शून्यक वे x-निर्देशांक हैं जहाँ y = p(x) का ग्राफ x-अक्ष को काटता है। रैखिक बहुपद का ग्राफ सरल रेखा होती है जो x-अक्ष को ठीक एक बिंदु पर काटती है — इसलिए एक शून्यक। द्विघात बहुपद का ग्राफ परवलय होता है जो x-अक्ष को दो, एक या शून्य बिंदुओं पर काट सकता है — इसलिए 2, 1 या 0 शून्यक। घन बहुपद के अधिकतम 3 शून्यक होते हैं। n घात के बहुपद के अधिकतम n शून्यक होते हैं।

instruction: 3x² + 5x – 2 के शून्यक ज्ञात कीजिए और शून्यकों तथा गुणांकों के संबंध की जाँच कीजिए।
रैखिक, द्विघात और घन बहुपद में क्या अंतर है?
response: बहुपदों को उनकी घात के आधार पर वर्गीकृत करते हैं: रैखिक बहुपद की घात 1 होती है, जैसे 2x – 3। इसका ग्राफ सरल रेखा होती है और इसका ठीक एक शून्यक होता है। द्विघात बहुपद की घात 2 होती है, जैसे x² – 3x – 4। इसका ग्राफ परवलय होता है और इसके 0, 1 या 2 शून्यक हो सकते हैं। घन बहुपद की घात 3 होती है, जैसे x³ – 4x। इसके अधिकतम 3 शून्यक होते हैं।

instruction: बहुपदाचे शून्य म्हणजे काय? उदाहरण द्या.
response: एखाद्या बहुपद p(x) चे शून्य म्हणजे ती वास्तविक संख्या k जिच्यासाठी p(k) = 0 होते. उदाहरणार्थ, p(x) = x² – 3x – 4 साठी p(–1) = 1 + 3 – 4 = 0, म्हणून –1 हे शून्य आहे. त्याचप्रमाणे p(4) = 0, म्हणून 4 हे देखील शून्य आहे. भूमितीय दृष्ट्या, बहुपदाचे शून्य हे x-अक्षाशी आलेखाच्या छेदनबिंदूंचे x-निर्देशांक असतात.

instruction: द्विघात बहुपदाचे शून्य आणि गुणांक यांच्यातील संबंध सांगा.
response: जर α आणि β हे ax² + bx + c या द्विघात बहुपदाचे शून्य असतील तर: शून्यांची बेरीज = α + β = –b/a = –(x चा गुणांक)/(x² चा गुणांक). शून्यांचा गुणाकार = αβ = c/a = (स्थिरांक पद)/(x² चा गुणांक). उदाहरण: 2x² – 8x + 6 चे शून्य 1 आणि 3 आहेत. बेरीज = 4 = 8/2 ✓. गुणाकार = 3 = 6/2 ✓.

instruction: घन बहुपदाला किती शून्य असू शकतात? भूमितीय स्पष्टीकरण द्या.
response: घन बहुपदाला जास्तीत जास्त 3 शून्य असू शकतात. भूमितीय दृष्ट्या, y = ax³ + bx² + cx + d चा आलेख x-अक्षाला जास्तीत जास्त 3 बिंदूंमध्ये छेदतो. उदाहरणार्थ, x³ – 4x चे शून्य –2, 0 आणि 2 आहेत — आलेख x-अक्षाला तीन बिंदूंवर छेदतो. x³ चे फक्त एकच शून्य (0) आहे. सर्वसाधारणपणे, n घाताच्या बहुपदाला जास्तीत जास्त n शून्य असतात.

instruction: रेषीय बहुपद ax + b चे शून्य काढा आणि उदाहरण द्या.
response: ax + b = 0 ठेवल्यास: ax = –b, म्हणजे x = –b/a. म्हणून रेषीय बहुपद ax + b चे शून्य = –b/a = –(स्थिरांक पद)/(x चा गुणांक). उदाहरण: p(x) = 2x + 3 साठी शून्य = –3/2. तपासणी: p(–3/2) = 2(–3/2) + 3 = –3 + 3 = 0 ✓. भूमितीय दृष्ट्या, हे y = 2x + 3 या सरळ रेषेने x-अक्षाला (–3/2, 0) या बिंदूवर छेदणे होय.

instruction: What is the maximum number of zeroes a polynomial of degree n can have? Why?
response: A polynomial of degree n can have at most n zeroes. This is because the graph of y = p(x) where p(x) has degree n can intersect the x-axis at most n points. For example: a linear polynomial (degree 1) has exactly 1 zero; a quadratic (degree 2) has at most 2 zeroes; a cubic (degree 3) has at most 3 zeroes. This is a fundamental property of polynomials. The word 'at most' is important — the polynomial x³ has degree 3 but only one zero (x = 0).

instruction: What is a parabola, and how does it relate to quadratic polynomials?
response: A parabola is the U-shaped curve formed by the graph of a quadratic polynomial y = ax² + bx + c. If a > 0, the parabola opens upward (like ∪). If a < 0, it opens downward (like ∩). The zeroes of the quadratic polynomial are exactly the x-coordinates of the points where the parabola crosses the x-axis. The parabola can cross the x-axis at two points (two zeroes), touch it at one point (one repeated zero), or stay completely above or below it (no real zeroes).

instruction: Find the zeroes of the cubic polynomial x³ – 4x and verify using the graph concept.
response: Factorise: x³ – 4x = x(x² – 4) = x(x – 2)(x + 2). Setting each factor to zero: x = 0, x = 2, x = –2. So the three zeroes are –2, 0, and 2. Verification using table: when x = –2, y = –8 + 8 = 0 ✓; when x = 0, y = 0 ✓; when x = 2, y = 8 – 8 = 0 ✓. Geometrically, the graph of y = x³ – 4x crosses the x-axis at exactly these three points, confirming our zeroes.

instruction: The graph of y = p(x) intersects the x-axis at 3 points. What is the minimum possible degree of p(x)?
response: If the graph of y = p(x) intersects the x-axis at 3 distinct points, then p(x) has 3 distinct zeroes. Since a polynomial of degree n has at most n zeroes, we need the degree to be at least 3 to accommodate 3 zeroes. A quadratic (degree 2) can have at most 2 zeroes, so it cannot produce 3 intersection points. Therefore, the minimum possible degree of p(x) is 3 — it must be at least a cubic polynomial.
